time.passTimeHint: Let time pass to the next part of the day

opening.subtitle: Eurbanities and Fragmentation
opening.selectHint: Who walks into the assembly tonight?
opening.name: Name

offline.title: Offline play
offline.unsupported: Cache API not available in this browser.

start.text: Good, you made it, {playerName}. The city map is not just about streets. It is about who can actually use them.

map.text: Look here. The same city creates different routes for different people.

start.text: Accessibility is not a special request. It is how the city becomes public.

choices.ask_barrier: Ask what barrier should be fixed first.

entrance.text: Start with the entrance. If people cannot enter, they cannot participate.

start.text: The Crossroads. Buses, stalls, shortcuts and queues: this is where the district decides who arrives, who waits and who gives up.
Main Bus Hub. Three lines by day, one after dinner. The timetable assumes nobody works evenings.

noted.text: You write it down. Evidence is how a complaint becomes a proposal.

start.text: The market is the district's living room: half the stalls take cash only, and the vendors know everyone's name.
A narrow crossing between parked cars. Fine on foot, a puzzle with a stroller, impossible in a wheelchair. Ben would have measured it already.
Civic Info Point. Opening hours: shorter than the queue. Still, the noticeboard is honest work.
Three interviews, three districts, no solutions in your pocket. If you come back with a proposal instead of three stories, you've done the wrong mission.

choices.engage: I'll go and listen. To the silences too.
choices.shortcut: I've already talked to people, I know what's needed.

resolution.text: (leafing through the empathy maps) Look: three neighbourhoods, three different ways of saying “nobody ever comes back”. That's where we start.

start.text: That crossing blocks children, older people and anyone on wheels — wheelchair or pram. The Council “will assess”; I've stopped waiting for verbs in the future tense.

choices.engage: We do the temporary fix AND the real proposal.
choices.shortcut: I'll report the problem to the technical office and be done.

resolution.text: (testing the ramp) It holds. And the difference between today and a ticket is that tonight Lia crosses on her own.

start.text: I can give you a photo with the mayoral sash, or a mandate with delegated powers and a budget. The first is free; the second you have to make defensible in council.

choices.engage: Here are names, dates and reviews.
choices.shortcut: Let's hold the event, the rest will sort itself out.

resolution.text: (signing) Know this: skip one review and the council will throw it in my face for years. (smiles) Don't skip it.

start.text: Thirty years ago that pier was one long party; now you want to “regenerate” it. Regenerate — conjugated from the verb to forget. (theatrical pause) Convince me otherwise.

choices.engage: Tell me about it. We put it inside the project, not in place of the project.

resolution.text: (at the dinner, moved) All right, all right. If memory serves to open the future instead of barring it… then growing old has been of some use.

start.text: One Saturday, twenty hands, zero money. Courtyard 17 can become a common room — or stay the place where the Corporate hangs his rendering: pick the Saturday.

choices.engage: I'll organise the crews and materials.
choices.shortcut: We'll call in a firm, sooner or later.

resolution.text: (wiping her hands) It's not finished — common things never are, you maintain them. That's the beauty of them.

start.text: I've got two drafts: “THE USUAL SUSPECTS' CENTER” — twelve thousand guaranteed clicks — or the true story, which is slower and less sexy. My editor wants the first by tonight; give me the second.

choices.engage: Here are the accounts, the photos and three people to interview. Check everything.
choices.shortcut: Write whatever you want, I don't have time.

resolution.text: (showing the piece) “Participation isn't dead: it changed address.” Fewer clicks, more true. (sighs) I hate it when you people improve me.

start.text: A hundred rumours pass through here every day — eighty true, twenty poisonous — and I sort them by ear. Help me turn this into a proper information point, without switching off the chatter: the chatter is half the neighbourhood.

choices.engage: A verified noticeboard, five languages, and your ear stays editor-in-chief.
choices.shortcut: A sign with the Council's opening hours will do.

resolution.text: (arranging the noticeboard) Look: notices in Urdu, Polish and Italian, and people's little slips underneath, same as ever. Order and warmth — like tea.

start.text: Everyone asks me to “turn a blind eye” — I have two, and a rulebook. But there is Article 9, collaboration pacts: nobody ever uses it because it takes work… fancy some?

choices.engage: We write the pact properly, clause by clause.
choices.shortcut: We just open it, we'll see later.

resolution.text: (stamping) Collaboration Pact no. 1. (almost smiling) Did you know that rules, used well, are quite moving?

start.text: The last usable run out of Hill Gardens is 19:40; whoever works the evening shift walks home, forty minutes, in the dark. I've had the numbers in my head for years — nobody's ever asked me for them.

choices.engage: I'm asking. Let's count a week of runs together.

resolution.text: (dossier on the dashboard) Twenty-three people on foot every night — now it's in writing. Let's see what weighs more at that table: my odometer or their Excel sheet.

start.text: I've done the sums: twelve active volunteers, four on the edge of burnout — if one collapses, everyone's shifts collapse. Caring for those who care isn't a luxury: it's maintenance of the network.

choices.engage: Shifts with mandatory rest, pairs, and the right to say no.
choices.shortcut: They're adults, they can pace themselves.

resolution.text: (pinning up the rota) “Resting is participating.” Piotr suggested that line — he's back.

start.text: Look at my bar: the pensioners' table, the kids' table, the newcomers' table — three islands. Same walls, zero words between them; I've got the space, what I'm missing is the excuse.

choices.engage: Theme nights and a shared noticeboard. We build the excuse together.
choices.shortcut: Put up a sign saying 'talk to each other'.

resolution.text: (gesturing at the room) Thursday: backgammon tournament, Donald versus the Youth Court kids. He lost and wants a rematch. (under his breath) When false rumours go round now, they fall apart on their own in here: people know each other.

start.text: Pots in the windows, a painted bench, an adopted flowerbed — small, yes. But a tended place says “someone is staying here”; what matters is that it stays in the residents' hands and doesn't become a shop window for tourists.

choices.engage: Minimal green, co-tended, with the names of those who look after it.
choices.shortcut: We'll call a garden centre and be done.

resolution.text: (watering with a little girl) There are three rules: who plants, tends; who tends, signs; who signs, tells the story. Beauty without keepers is only scenery.

start.text: You adults measure streets in metres; I measure them in “where I can go on my own”. Want to see my map? It's full of holes.

choices.engage: You lead. I'll write down everything you say.

resolution.text: (at the noticeboard, showing the pinned-up map) That red X — I put it there. And they copied it into the real document. (to Zoe) See? Sometimes they do listen to us.

start.text: Everyone talks about the climate in the future tense; I have Lake Edge's drains full of leaves now, and a list of low houses that go under after two hours of rain. Prevention is boring — that's why nobody does it.

choices.engage: A channel-clearing day + evacuation list + assembly point. Boring stuff — let's do it.
choices.shortcut: If it happens, we'll call civil protection.

resolution.text: (watching the sky) Now we're even allowed to hope it won't be needed. Hoping AFTER you've cleared the channels is a different kind of hope altogether.

start.text: I taught for forty years; now I watch intelligent adults sit silent in meetings for fear of a grammar mistake. Language is the city's first door — I still have the key, if anyone is interested.

choices.engage: A language and citizenship course. And your former pupils as tutors.
choices.shortcut: There are apps for learning languages.

resolution.text: (after the first lesson) Mrs Anh asked a question in public today. Wrong subjunctive, perfect courage. (adjusting her glasses) We shall correct the subjunctive.

start.text: The papers only ever cover Hill Gardens in the crime pages; I can draw, and the kids at the wall know what needs drawing — it's called counter-narrative. But if I'm the only one signing it, that's just another theft.

choices.engage: Co-creation: the residents choose the stories, you draw them, you sign together.
choices.shortcut: You do it, you're the artist.

resolution.text: (in front of the mural signed by 14 hands) My name is the smallest one. I've never been this happy about a bad placement.

start.text: I deliver even where the doorbells have lost their names. Mr Ferro, third floor — I'm the only voice he hears all week, and I know twenty more like him: no list holds them.

choose.text: Here they are: older people alone, newcomers. What do we do?

choices.connect: Then we make the list ourselves. Discreetly, and with a face for every name.

resolution.text: (nearly weeping) Mr Ferro made coffee for two neighbours. Twenty-three years without opening his door to anyone — twenty-three! (blows his nose) Excuse me, it's the dust.

start.text: (flat voice, contract already printed) Full refurbishment, ninety days, twenty-year management to us — the neighbourhood gets a beautiful space, and the use, well… gets regulated. Sign here.

choices.accept: Sign the contract. Ninety days, a beautiful space.
choices.negotiate: Countersign the civic pact: guaranteed public hours, social tariffs, an annual review.
choices.refuse: We already have ninety neighbours. We don't need ninety days.

resolution.text: (closing the briefcase, without rancour) Interesting. Tired cities usually sign. (at the door) We'll be back when you're tired.

P01.title: The Center Holds?
P01.description: Understand why the Community Center is losing its civic function.

objectives.talk_to_anna: Talk to Anna about the civic map.

P02.title: Same City, Different Routes
P02.description: Discover how the same district creates different routes for different people.

C01.title: Arriving Is Already Participation
C01.description: Investigate mobility barriers around the Crossroads.

N01.title: Listen Before Fixing
N01.description: Interview at least three people in different districts before proposing solutions.

objectives.interview_three: Listen to 3 people in different districts.

N02.title: The Crossing
N02.description: Make safe a crossing that blocks children, older people and disabled residents.

N03.title: Mandate, Not a Photo Op
N03.description: Turn political attention into a real commitment with clear responsibilities.

objectives.secure_mandate: Secure a commitment with defined responsibilities.

N04.title: Memory Is Not Nostalgia
N04.description: Use the neighbourhood's memory to legitimise the new instead of blocking it.

objectives.collect_memory: Gather memory and link it to a new proposal.

N05.title: The Repair Day
N05.description: Collective repair of a space with limited resources (Modular Repair mini-game).

N06.title: The Story Behind the Headline
N06.description: Choose whether Ruben fuels or defuses the false rumour.

objectives.provide_material: Provide authentic material for the story.

N07.title: From Rumor to Information
N07.description: Turn the newsstand into a reliable, multilingual info point.

objectives.setup_info: Set up the multilingual info point.

N08.title: Rules and Open Doors
N08.description: Find a legal way to open a common room (Commons Access mini-game).

objectives.agreement: Establish a use agreement with responsibilities.

N09.title: The Last Bus
N09.description: Document a missing connection and build a proposal.

N10.title: Care for the Carers
N10.description: Introduce rest practices and load sharing in the network.

N11.title: Nobody Is Off the Map
N11.description: Find isolated residents and connect them to a care network.

objectives.connect: Connect them to a care network.

N12.title: Mixed Tables
N12.description: Turn the bar into a meeting place for separate groups.

objectives.mixed_tables: Organise theme nights and a shared noticeboard.

N13.title: Care Made Visible
N13.description: Minimal green co-tended by residents, staying in their hands.

N14.title: Through a Child's Eyes
N14.description: Map the city from the children's point of view.

N15.title: Before the Water Rises
N15.description: Prepare networks and routes before the climate crisis (Crisis Response Map mini-game).

N16.title: Words to Belong
N16.description: Language and intergenerational memory course to truly participate.

N17.title: Don't Tell Our Story Without Us
N17.description: Counter-narrative co-created with residents and young people (Story Routes mini-game).

N18.title: The Offer
N18.description: Anti-quest: accept, negotiate or refuse the speculative offer.

objectives.decide_offer: Decide how to answer the offer.

p1.title: Eurbania
p1.text: A mid-sized European city, like many others. Trams that are almost on time, squares that fill and empty, neighbours who nod without quite knowing each other's names.

p2.title: The Fragmentation
p2.text: Lately something has been pulling the city apart. Not a storm, not a crisis on the news — just distance. People retreating indoors, shared spaces going quiet, decisions made about everyone by fewer and fewer.

p3.title: The Community Center
p3.text: One place still tries to hold the threads together. Its doors are open, its noticeboard is full, and tonight it is busier than usual: the city has called an assembly, and someone has to show up.

p4.text: Five people could walk through that door. Tonight, one of them is you. Who will you be?

maya.tagline: A shift worker and Zoe's mother. For her the city is a puzzle of schedules that barely fit.

samir.tagline: A courier who knows every shortcut and every closed door.

elena.tagline: A Town Hall official who must turn listening into real decisions, without overpromising.

luca.tagline: A small business owner trying to serve the neighbourhood without going under.

custom.tagline: Your own route through Eurbania: choose who to help, knowing you can't help everyone.

heatwave.announce: Heat alert: 38° forecast. Isolated elders and fragile housing at risk in districts without shade. GIORGIA: “I have the Old Blocks vulnerable list… two years out of date.”
heatwave.reactive: Water gets handed out wherever. By evening an elderly man is taken to hospital — the logbook records his name.
heatwave.coordinated: The Center opens as a cool refuge, with shifts. MAYA (evening, tired): “Zoe did her homework in the courtyard today with six other kids. It's 36 degrees and it was… nice?”
heatwave.transformative: Pablo's door-to-door network and the neighbourhood cool rooms hold the day — the permanent “cool map” is born. PABLO: “Mine is from yesterday — twenty-three names, floor by floor. Let's go.”

rumor.announce: Viral post: “EXCLUSIVE: Community Center funds go only to SOME groups. Guess which ones.” SAMIR: “The ‘which ones’ is me, isn't it?”
rumor.reactive: A denial press release goes out. Nobody reads it.
rumor.coordinated: Public accounts on the noticeboard and an open clarification assembly. Trust steadies.
rumor.transformative: Abdullah's info kiosk publishes the numbers in five languages; Ruben signs the piece that dismantles the rumour with sources; Marta turns it into a comic strip on the wall. ABDULLAH: “The rumour ran. But this time the truth had a kiosk, and it was open.”

offer.announce: The Mystery Corporate Man turns up at the Town Hall with a rendering: “GREY YARDS SQUARE — opening in 90 days. Zero cost to the Council. Sign, and the problem disappears.” ALEXANDRIA: “Give me a credible alternative today.”
offer.reactive: A refusal on principle, with no alternative. The Offer will return, stronger.
offer.coordinated: Public conditions are negotiated: guaranteed hours, social tariffs. The commons hold, but don't grow.
offer.transformative: A civic stewardship pact answers the rendering: Tom's regulation, Sigrid's works, the traders' fund. The Corporate Man withdraws: “We'll be back when you're tired.”

closure.announce: Notice: “Temporary suspension: evening bus line 14 + Lake Edge after-school room. Resource rationalisation.” GWEN: “‘Temporary’. Like Viveca's Thursdays, three years ago.”
closure.reactive: A petition circulates. Signatures without data get filed away.
closure.coordinated: Gwen's real counts reach the committee with Elena. The line is saved on a reduced timetable.
closure.transformative: Three riders tell the committee what the last bus means — the resolution quotes their words. The after-school club reopens in the courtyard, Viveca volunteering. ZOE: “The new teacher is exactly as strict as the old one.”

flood.announce: Torrential rain over Lake Edge and Old Blocks. Teams, sandbags, assembly points: who goes to whom? BEN: “The new crossing is the only dry route for wheelchairs. I told you it wasn't ‘a detail’.”
flood.reactive: Pure emergency. A district sinks into fragmentation.
flood.coordinated: The networks activate; the damage is contained.
flood.transformative: The response becomes protocol — the “Water Plan” enters the civic pact. ANNA (that evening, in the half-flooded but crowded Center): “Look at yourselves — THIS is the network. Tomorrow we put it in writing.”
